name: Triple Pear Puree
recipeIngredient: 6 pounds Anjou pears (each cut into 1/8ths), peeled, cored
2/3 cup poire William or pear brandy
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the pears and water in a heavy large saucepan.
2. Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
3. Stir occasionally until the pears are very soft, about 40 minutes.
4. Remove from heat and transfer the mixture to a blender.
5. Blend in batches until smooth.
6. Return the pureed mixture to the saucepan.
7. Mix in the pear vinegar, sugar, and poire William or pear brandy.
8. Simmer over medium-low heat for about 40 minutes, stirring frequently, until reduced by one-third and thickened to the consistency of applesauce.
9. Serve at room temperature.
Note: Triple Pear Puree can be prepared up to 2 days in advance and stored refrigerated.
